author: draganrakita
content:
Example:
* call has: 2M regular gas, 2M reservoir
* It calls a subcall with (1M regular gas, and 2M reservoir).
* Subcalls spends 2.3M state gas, and 0.1M regular gas (This means all 2M of reservoir and 0.4M of regular gas)
    * This means 0.4M of regular gas contains 0.3M state gas + 0.1M cpu gas 
* Revert/halt happen.
* Question what value for parent call should reservoir be refilled with.

Expectation on revert case (state reservoir refilled) do we expect :
* parent call to have 1.6M regular gas, and 2M of reservoir. 0.3 of state gas was deducted but it is not spent
* parent call to have 1.6M regualr gas, and 2.3M of reservoir. 0.3 og state gas gets added to reservoir

Expectation on halt case (state reservoir refilled) do we expect:
* parent call to have 1M regular gas, and 2M of reservoir. 0.3 of state gas was deducted but it is not spent
* parent call to have 1M regualr gas, and 2.3M of reservoir. 0.3 og state gas gets added to reservoir

author: draganrakita
content: Idea is to preserve state gas, but dont effect the common regular gas behavior on halt/revert.  In we take first expectation, we are having a fake spending of state gas.
